page: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU family server configured for accelerated compute workloads. The system is powered by 12 shared Intel Core physical cores based on the Broadwell architecture, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. This processing core setup is paired with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 10.67 GB of RAM per core.

Graphics and parallel computing acceleration are delivered by two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs, which provide a combined total of 32 GB of VRAM. For local storage, the server is equipped with a 700 GB NVMe SSD, facilitating high-speed read and write operations. Additionally, the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In synthetic CPU benchmarks, the instance demonstrates mid-tier performance. It records a stress-ng single-core score of 1754.67 and a multi-core score of 21080.54, both falling within the average performance category. While the CPU supports AVX, AES cryptography, and FMA instructions, it lacks newer instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AMX.

Given its hardware profile, this server offers a cost-effective option for GPU-accelerated workloads that require substantial memory and local NVMe storage without the expense of high-end enterprise accelerators. It is suitable for virtual desktop infrastructure (VDI) hosting, media streaming, video transcoding, and moderate machine learning inference.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU family server designed for accelerated workloads. It is equipped with 12 shared Intel Core (Broadwell) physical cores running at 2.0 GHz, 128.0 GB of system memory, and a 700 GB NVMe SSD. Graphics and compute acceleration are provided by two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with a total of 32 GB of VRAM. In synthetic benchmarks, the server delivers average-tier CPU performance, with stress-ng scores of 1754.67 for single-core and 21080.54 for multi-core operations. The hardware profile lacks AVX-512 and AMX extensions but supports AVX, AES, and FMA. This server offers a cost-effective balance of high memory capacity, local NVMe storage, and dual-GPU acceleration, making it well-suited for VDI deployment, media transcoding, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU server featuring 12 Intel cores, 128 GB RAM, a 700 GB NVMe SSD, and two NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 32 GB VRAM for accelerated workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-12c-128g-32vram is a Cloud GPU server with 12 Intel cores, 128 GB RAM, two NVIDIA A16 GPUs, and 700 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An accelerated cloud server featuring dual NVIDIA Ampere GPUs, high memory capacity, and fast NVMe storage for graphics and compute workloads.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPUs with 32 GB total VRAM
12 shared Intel Broadwell cores at 2.0 GHz
128 GB system memory providing 10.67 GB per core
Suitable for VDI, media transcoding, and machine learning inference
